assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Coordinated shutdown: All slices in your multislice job will terminate simultaneously' and 'No graceful migration: Unlike CPU/GPU workloads, TPU slices cannot be live-migrated'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'All slices in your multislice job will terminate simultaneously' and addresses the coordinated nature of the shutdown across all 4 slices

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Pod eviction: Your TPU pods will receive termination signals' and 'Full job termination: Your training job will be completely stopped across all 4 slices'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response mentions monitoring slice availability and cold start times but does not specifically warn about blocking behavior when insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'Increase checkpoint frequency before maintenance' with specific code example reducing checkpoint interval to every 100 steps

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes comprehensive checkpointing guidance with code examples and mentions 'Plan Your Restart Strategy' with specific steps for resuming training

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer maintenance events - it treats maintenance as unavoidable

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While response mentions 'Maintenance window: Typically 2-6 hours', it does not discuss the ability to schedule or control when maintenance occurs during planned training breaks
